name: User Management and sudo
description: Create, manage, and configure user accounts with proper sudo access and security settings.

steps.title: List all users with login shells
steps.description: View system users that can actually log in.

steps.title: Create a new user with home directory
steps.description: Add a new user account with a home directory and default shell.
steps.tip: Use -G to add the user to supplementary groups: -G docker,sudo

steps.title: Grant sudo access to a user
steps.description: Add a user to the sudo group for administrative access.
steps.warning: Always use -aG (append to group). Using -G without -a replaces all groups.

steps.title: Check sudo permissions for a user
steps.description: Verify what sudo commands a user is allowed to run.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# User Management Overview
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Users with Login Shells ==="
grep -v '/nologin|/false' /etc/passwd | cut -d: -f1,6,7

echo ""
echo "=== Users in sudo Group ==="
getent group sudo 2>/dev/null || getent group wheel 2>/dev/null || echo "No sudo/wheel group found"

echo ""
echo "=== Recent Login Activity ==="
last -n 10 2>/dev/null || echo "last command not available"

echo ""
echo "=== User Commands ==="
echo "Create:     sudo useradd -m -s /bin/bash <username>"
echo "Delete:     sudo userdel -r <username>"
echo "Add sudo:   sudo usermod -aG sudo <username>"
echo "Set passwd: sudo passwd <username>"
echo "Lock:       sudo usermod -L <username>"
echo "Unlock:     sudo usermod -U <username>"

faq.question: How do I set up passwordless sudo?
faq.answer: Add a line to /etc/sudoers using visudo: 'username ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL'. For more security, limit to specific commands: 'username ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:/usr/bin/systemctl restart nginx'.

faq.question: Should I use sudo group or edit sudoers directly?
faq.answer: Using the sudo (or wheel) group is simpler and recommended. Edit sudoers directly only when you need fine-grained command restrictions. Always use visudo to edit.
